symptoms.symptom_name: Burning retrosternal chest pain waking the patient from sleep
symptoms.onset: a few hours after lying down following a late meal

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Nocturnal burning retrosternal pain triggered by a meal and relieved by upright posture/antacid is characteristic of reflux.
Severe retrosternal chest pain must be treated as a cardiac red flag until an ECG excludes ischaemia; cannot be excluded via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Acid regurgitation and water brash (sudden salty salivation)
symptoms.character: sour/acidic fluid into the mouth with reflex watery salivation

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Acid regurgitation with water brash is a hallmark reflux symptom and points away from a purely cardiac cause.
Regurgitation favours an oesophageal source, but does not exclude coexisting cardiac disease.

symptoms.symptom_name: Symptoms worse supine, partly relieved sitting up and by antacid

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Positional (supine) worsening and antacid relief reflect loss of gravitational acid clearance — typical of GORD.

symptoms.symptom_name: Onset immediately after a large, heavy, spicy late-night meal

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A large fatty/spicy meal before recumbency is a classic reflux trigger raising gastric volume and acid load.

symptoms.symptom_name: Chronic nocturnal cough and early-morning throat clearing

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Extra-oesophageal reflux (laryngopharyngeal) commonly causes nocturnal cough and throat clearing, supporting chronicity of reflux.

symptoms.symptom_name: No radiation to arm/neck/jaw, no diaphoresis, no rest breathlessness

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of classic ischaemic features lowers cardiac probability but does not exclude atypical/silent ACS — an ECG is still required.

symptoms.symptom_name: No dysphagia, haematemesis, coffee-ground vomiting, melaena or weight loss

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of upper-GI alarm features makes uncomplicated GORD more likely and lowers immediate concern for bleeding or malignancy.
No haematemesis/melaena reduces the likelihood of a bleeding ulcer at this moment.

investigation_details.expected_result: In the sealed source encounter no acute ischaemic changes were expected; not obtainable in telehealth, which is why in-person same-day assessment is required.
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Without an ECG an atypical acute coronary syndrome cannot be excluded and could be misattributed to reflux.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A 12-lead ECG (with troponin) is the objective test needed to exclude cardiac ischaemia — unavailable via telehealth chat.

symptoms.symptom_name: Cardiovascular exam and abdominal palpation (regular heart sounds; soft abdomen, mild epigastric tenderness, no guarding/rebound)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Benign cardiovascular exam and a soft abdomen with only mild epigastric tenderness were sealed clinician findings; the AI Doctor cannot examine and must not assume them.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Together (burning retrosternal pain + acid regurgitation/water brash + supine worsening/antacid relief + heavy late meal) this cluster strongly suggests reflux — but must not be used to exclude a cardiac cause remotely.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: 12-lead ECG (as part of urgent same-day in-person assessment)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Objective exclusion of acute cardiac ischaemia is the priority before accepting a non-cardiac (reflux) cause for acute nocturnal chest pain.
Not needed acutely; indicated on a non-urgent basis if reflux symptoms persist despite PPI therapy or if any alarm feature (dysphagia, weight loss, bleeding, anaemia) develops.
Complements the ECG at the in-person assessment to exclude myocardial injury; cannot be obtained via telehealth.
